channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Anthropic’s Claude reports widespread outage
item.description: Anthropic's AI chatbot Claude experienced widespread service disruptions on Monday morning, with thousands of users reporting issues accessing the bot.

item.title: What Happens When Your Hackathon Has Less Than 24 Hours Left and Your Backend Isn't Built
item.description: "A honest account of the decisions that actually matter when the clock is running." There is a moment in every hackathon where the project you planned to build and the project you are actually...

item.title: The Two Problems Nobody Owns in AI: Accessibility and Security Are Design Problems in Disguise
item.description: "You should always know what is yours. In your product, in your model, in your team." There is a quiet crisis in AI development that nobody talks about directly, because it lives in the gap...

item.title: How to Visualize and Style Routes on a Leaflet Map
item.description: Displaying a route on a map is straightforward. Making it look good takes more thought. A plain blue line works, but professional applications use shadows, custom colors, and polished markers to...

item.title: The Vision: A Living Map of the Machine 🌐
item.description: At oourmind.io Lab, a capsule created for Mistral March 2026 Hackathon, we believe AI shouldn't be a "black box" that we simply fear or blindly trust. Current interpretability research often...

item.title: Azure Weekly: OpenAI's GPT-5.3-Codex, Agentic Cloud Ops, and MCP Everywhere
item.description: The Week Azure Went All-In on Agents This past week saw some of the most substantial Azure releases in months — three new OpenAI models optimized for production use, agentic cloud operations...

item.title: 🧠 The 48-Hour Blueprint: Architecting a 3D Interpretability Lab for Mistral Large 3
item.description: Abstract (The "Elevator Pitch"): Most AI interfaces treat LLMs like chat-boxes. We believe they are a Society of Minds. In 48 hours, we are building OourMind.io, a multi-sensory interpretability...

item.title: Copilot CLI Weekly: General Availability Has Landed
item.description: The Preview Is Over GitHub announced general availability of Copilot CLI on February 25, 2026. After five months of public preview since September 2025, the terminal-native coding agent is now...

item.title: VS Code Weekly: The Browser Just Joined Your Agent Team
item.description: Native Browsers, Now With Agent Supervision Microsoft dropped the February 2026 Insiders build (v1.110) with daily updates throughout the month, and one feature stands out: native browser...

item.title: GitHub Weekly: Agentic Workflows Hit Tech Preview, Gemini 3.1 Pro Arrives
item.description: The Big Story: Agentic Workflows Go Public This week, GitHub launched Agentic Workflows in technical preview—the most significant platform update since GitHub Actions itself. This isn't just...

item.title: PEP 827's Complex Type Manipulation: Balancing Python's Static Typing Expansion with Ecosystem Usability
item.description: Introduction: PEP 827 and the Tightrope of Type Complexity PEP 827 has landed, and with it, a seismic shift in Python's static typing landscape. This proposal introduces a barrage of type...

item.title: Pokémon Pokopia is an expansive adventure disguised as a cozy life sim
item.description: Nintendo has a history of fleshing out the larger Pokémon world through spinoffs. What games from the Pokémon Snap and Detective Pikachu series lacked in terms of action, they made up for in the...

item.title: I checked out one of the biggest anti-AI protests ever
item.description: Pull the plug! Pull the plug! Stop the slop! Stop the slop! For a few hours this Saturday, February 28, I watched as a couple hundred anti-AI protesters marched through London’s King’s Cross tech...

item.title: LLM-Assisted Deanonymization
item.description: Turns out that LLMs are good at de-anonymization: We show that LLM agents can figure out who you are from your anonymous online posts. Across Hacker News, Reddit, LinkedIn, and anonymized...

item.title: How to Protect Your SaaS from Bot Attacks with SafeLine WAF
item.description: Most SaaS teams remember the day their user traffic started growing fast. Few notice the day bots started targeting them. On paper, everything looks great: more sign-ups, more sessions, more API...

item.title: Oh great, here comes 6G
item.description: It brings me no pleasure to say this, but friends: The next G cometh. The standard formally known as 6G is still being established, but it's going to be a major topic of discussion at Mobile World...

item.title: APT28 Tied to CVE-2026-21513 MSHTML 0-Day Exploited Before Feb 2026 Patch Tuesday
item.description: A recently disclosed security flaw patched by Microsoft may have been exploited by the Russia-linked state-sponsored threat actor known as APT28, according to new findings from Akamai. The...

item.title: The Data Centers Have Arrived at the Edge of the Arctic Circle
item.description: As AI labs gorge themselves on compute, data center operators have headed north in search of cheap and plentiful energy.

item.title: Tecno’s latest concept phone is lit by neon
item.description: After teasing a magnetic, modular smartphone concept the other day, Tecno has followed that up with another two phone designs centered around color-changing finishes: one with E Ink, and the other...

item.title: North Korean Hackers Publish 26 npm Packages Hiding Pastebin C2 for Cross-Platform RAT
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a new iteration of the ongoing Contagious Interview campaign, where the North Korean threat actors have published a set of 26 malicious packages to the npm...

item.title: Vivo’s next phone will launch with a professional camera rig
item.description: Vivo rarely has a presence at MWC, but this year it bucked that trend in a big way, with the reveal of its next flagship phone, the X300 Ultra, alongside an upgraded telephoto extender lens and...

item.title: Qualcomm’s new chip is geared toward wearable AI gadgets
item.description: Like it or not, more AI wearables may be on the horizon - or at least Qualcomm seems to think so. Today the company announced its new Snapdragon Wear Elite chip. At a press briefing, Qualcomm said...

item.title: The Motorola Razr Fold is shaping up to be pure flagship
item.description: Motorola has revealed a little more about its upcoming folding phone, and all signs point toward a premium option - not the budget-friendlier alternative the category could use. For starters, the...

item.title: A robot arm with puppy dog eyes is just one of Lenovo’s new desktop AI concepts
item.description: Alongside a handful of new laptop concepts (and a range of real products too), Lenovo used MWC to announce a pair of AI-based productivity companion concepts. Both are standalone desk devices...

item.title: The new Yoga 9i 2-in-1 from Lenovo has an angled ‘canvas mode’ for easier note-taking
item.description: Lenovo has a few new Yoga laptops it's announcing at MWC 2026: the 14-inch Yoga Slim 7i Aura Edition, 15-inch Yoga Pro 7a, and 14-inch Yoga 9i 2-in-1 Aura Edition. The most interesting of the pack...

item.title: Lenovo’s redesigned ThinkPad Detachable tablet has a bigger screen and legit keyboard
item.description: Lenovo is announcing five new ThinkPads and a new ThinkBook laptop for MWC 2026. There are various new chip offerings and updates for this swathe of ThinkPads, but the device I find the most...

item.title: Lenovo made a Framework-like laptop with modular ports — and a second screen
item.description: One of Lenovo's big laptop concepts for MWC 2026 is a modular ThinkBook with two screens. Officially called the ThinkBook Modular AI PC Concept, the proof-of-concept is a 14-inch productivity...

item.title: Google looks to tackle longstanding RCS spam in India — but not alone
item.description: Google is integrating carrier-level filtering into RCS in India through a partnership with Airtel to strengthen protections against spam.

item.title: OpenAI reveals more details about its agreement with the Pentagon
item.description: By CEO Sam Altman’s own admission, OpenAI’s deal with the Department of Defense was “definitely rushed,” and “the optics don’t look good.”

item.title: Anthropic’s Claude rises to No. 1 in the App Store following Pentagon dispute
item.description: Anthropic’s chatbot Claude seems to have benefited from the attention around the company’s fraught negotiations with the Pentagon.
